notes: Suppresses call-type rubric items that are already owned by the universal NREMT base rubric. ALS-request applicability is resolved from active agency configuration, not scenario overlay content.

operations.reason: Universal NREMT base rubric owns PPE and scene safety via scene_entry evidence path. The call-type item uses the same scene_entry_path but does not credit even when the scene entry popup is completed, creating a false miss alongside the credited base item.
operations.protocol_ref: NREMT EMT Patient Assessment/Management Medical E202 — scene safety and PPE are base rubric items

operations.reason: This authored croup scenario already sends ALS/EMS by dispatch/turnover context. Do not score the learner for explicitly requesting an intercept; DMIST covers communication of weight, stridor severity, SpO2, and response to BLS care.
operations.protocol_ref: Scenario dispatch/turnover context — ALS request is not a learner action when advanced care is already dispatched
